mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate hike periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0%, it signals a breaking labor market that reduces consumer spending power and corporate profit growth, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in 70% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.02 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in consumer price inflation and deceleration in retail sales indicates stagflationary pressure that compresses corporate margins and triggers equity sell-offs over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when CPIAUCSL rises and RSAFS falls, with a combined Rank IC below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of aggressive monetary tightening, a monthly decline in the industrial production index reflects immediate supply chain constraints and reduced manufacturing output, causing short-term negative equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases, invalidating the short-term supply shock transmission.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate wage income and consumer demand, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth consistently fails to predict positive 20-day returns, with a walk-forward Rank IC remaining below 0.02 across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts cross below 1.2 million units, it signals a severe contraction in construction activity and related consumer spending, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in over 65% of months where HOUST remains below 1200.0, or the threshold effect disappears in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: High policy rates combined with rising unemployment indicate that monetary tightening is successfully cooling the economy, which reduces recession risk premiums and produces positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction of high FEDFUNDS and rising UNRATE yields negative 20-day returns in 70% of cases, or the joint predictive power falls below Rank IC 0.01 in rolling tests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above 2.0% confirms robust economic expansion that sustains corporate earnings trajectories, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth above 2.0% is followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of quarters, or the signal shows no statistical edge (p > 0.10) in walk-forward analysis.
